codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers all required feature behaviors: NDJSON append-only logging at audit/swell-actions.ndjson, sha256 hash chaining with prev_hash linking, tool-call/external-write/decision/state-transition/session-start/session-end event logging, and chain integrity verification. All 10 unit tests pass. The implementation correctly uses swell-core as the home for audit functionality per architecture.md component boundaries.

issues.description: The skill defines additional event kinds not implemented: 'credential-access' (secret/key accessed), 'install-extend' (package install/skill activation), 'payment' (ACTP/x402 payment), and 'ingress' (external input received as standalone kind). These appear to be AGIRAILS-platform-specific rather than core EU AI Act Article 12 requirements, but if the agent-audit-trail skill is meant to be followed verbatim, these would be gaps.

AuditLog::verify_chain() re-reads the entire file from disk each time. For a high-volume audit log, this could be slow. However, for MVP this is acceptable and the function correctly implements chain verification.

The skill references 'audit/atlas-actions.ndjson' while the implementation uses 'audit/swell-actions.ndjson'. This is correct for the SWELL project but represents a naming convention discrepancy from the skill. The AGENTS.md does not document the expected audit log file path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The agent-audit-trail skill specifies audit log path as 'audit/atlas-actions.ndjson' but SWELL uses 'audit/swell-actions.ndjson'. Neither AGENTS.md nor .factory/library/ specify the expected audit log naming convention. Worker correctly used 'swell-actions.ndjson' which is appropriate for this project, but there's no documented convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file: audit/atlas-actions.ndjson; Implementation: audit/swell-actions.ndjson (line 23 of audit.rs: const AUDIT_FILE: &str = "audit/swell-actions.ndjson")

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill defines 11 event kinds but only 8 are implemented. The missing kinds (credential-access, install-extend, payment, ingress) appear specific to the AGIRAILS payment platform the skill was designed for. Worker followed procedure correctly - no deviations noted.

Worker had to determine appropriate crate location (swell-core) for audit functionality. The architecture.md defines swell-core as containing 'Traits, types, errors' but doesn't explicitly mention audit/logging as a concern. Worker correctly placed audit.rs in swell-core alongside traits.rs and types.rs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Architecture.md: swell-core provides 'Traits, types, errors'; Worker created audit.rs in crates/swell-core/src/

summary: PASS - Implementation fully satisfies feature requirements for EU AI Act Article 12 hash-chained audit trail. All expected behaviors (NDJSON logging, sha256 hash chaining, tool-call/external-write/decision/state-transition/session events, chain verification) are implemented and tested. Non-blocking observations: skill defines additional AGIRAILS-specific event kinds not implemented, and audit log file naming convention is not documented in shared state.
